name: Code Review Scenarios
description: You're reviewing a PR — can you spot the issue?

questions.question: What issue would you flag in this code review?
options: The component should destructure users with a default empty array to prevent crashes
Rendering list items without a wrapper fragment causes React hydration mismatch errors
Each <li> rendered inside .map() is missing a unique key prop needed for reconciliation
The user.name property access requires optional chaining to handle null values safely
questions.explanation: React requires a unique 'key' prop on elements rendered inside .map() to efficiently track list changes. Without it, React may re-render the entire list unnecessarily.

questions.question: What's the problem with this useEffect?
options: The useEffect callback should be defined as a named async function for proper teardown
The document.title assignment should use a ref to avoid triggering unwanted side effects
The useState initialization value should match the current document title on first render
The dependency array is empty but references count, so the title won't update on changes
questions.explanation: The effect uses 'count' but the dependency array is empty []. This means the title only updates on mount, not when count changes. Add 'count' to the dependency array.

questions.question: Why won't this trigger a re-render?
options: The array is mutated in place so setItems receives the same reference and skips re-render
The push method returns the new length instead of the array, causing setItems to store a number
Calling setItems inside a synchronous handler blocks React's reconciliation batching cycle
The useState initializer with an array literal creates a new reference on every render pass
questions.explanation: Calling items.push() mutates the existing array. Since setItems receives the same reference, React doesn't detect a change. Use setItems([...items, 'c']) to create a new array.

questions.question: What potential performance issue exists here?
options: The arrow function captures stale closure variables from the parent component scope
Inline handlers bypass React's synthetic event pooling mechanism and reduce performance
A new function reference is created each render, breaking memoization of child components
Passing a callback with arguments prevents proper event delegation on the DOM element
questions.explanation: The inline arrow function creates a new function reference on every render. If this button is in a memoized child component, it will break memoization. Use useCallback or extract the handler.

questions.question: What critical issue is in this useEffect?
options: The subscription is never cleaned up on unmount, causing a memory leak and stale updates
The subscribe callback should use the functional form of setMessages to avoid stale state
Placing an async subscription inside useEffect without await causes unhandled rejections
The dependency array should include api and setMessages to track all external references
questions.explanation: The subscription is never cleaned up. When the component unmounts, the callback still fires, causing memory leaks and state updates on unmounted components. Return a cleanup: return () => sub.unsubscribe();

questions.question: What TypeScript issue would you flag?
options: The function return type should be explicitly annotated as Promise<User> for contracts
Template literal URL construction should use a URL builder to prevent type-unsafe paths
The res variable should be typed as Response instead of relying on implicit inference
Using 'any' annotations on all types removes the type safety that TypeScript provides
questions.explanation: Using 'any' everywhere removes all type safety. Define an interface for the user, type the parameter as string/number, and type the response. Also, res.json() needs 'await'.

questions.question: What CSS code smell is present?
options: Cascading !important declarations create specificity conflicts that are very hard to maintain
The selectors use increasingly complex combinators that reduce overall CSS parsing performance
Named color values like red, blue, and green render inconsistently across different browsers
Each class override should be scoped inside a CSS module to prevent global style leakage
questions.explanation: When !important is used on every rule, you end up fighting specificity with more specificity. Refactor to use a clear, low-specificity naming convention like BEM instead.

questions.question: What's missing from this async function?
options: The function should validate the response status code before attempting to parse JSON
The setData call should be wrapped in startTransition to prevent UI jank on slow networks
There is no try/catch or error handling, so a failed request silently breaks the UI state
The fetch call needs an AbortController signal to cancel the request on component unmount
questions.explanation: There's no try/catch or .catch() handling. If the network request fails or returns a non-OK status, the error is unhandled and the UI may break silently. Always add error handling for async operations.

questions.question: What architectural issue does this component tree show?
options: The deeply nested component tree slows down React's reconciliation and diffing passes
Each component should fetch its own user data instead of relying on a passed-down prop
Layout and Sidebar should be combined into a single compound component to reduce nesting
The user prop is drilled through every level even though intermediary components don't use it
questions.explanation: The 'user' prop is drilled through 4 levels. Use React Context, composition (children pattern), or a state management library to avoid passing props through intermediary components that don't use them.

questions.question: What accessibility issues are present?
options: The SVG element inside the button should have a role attribute defining its semantic purpose
The image is missing alt text and the icon-only button has no accessible label for screen readers
Using lowercase onclick instead of onClick prevents the handler from binding correctly in JSX
The button element needs an explicit tabIndex attribute to be reachable via keyboard navigation
questions.explanation: The needs an alt attribute for screen readers. The icon-only button has no accessible label — add aria-label='Buy' so assistive technologies can describe its purpose.
